# llm-search-mediator

> Privacy-preserving search bridge for local LLMs.

llm-search-mediator is a Python/Flask HTTP service that sits between an LLM
and a SearXNG metasearch backend. It sanitizes outbound queries (PII stripping),
applies differential privacy (decoys, query generalization, batch timing),
protects against traffic analysis (random jitter, fixed-size padding), filters
inbound results for prompt injection, and audit-logs every decision.

## What it does

- Accepts `POST /v1/search` with `{"query": "...", "categories": "general"}`
- Strips PII (email, phone, SSN, credit card, IP, API keys, hex tokens)
- Blocks queries that are >50% redacted PII
- Checks for identifying patterns (proper names, addresses, case numbers)
- Sends decoy searches and cover queries for differential privacy
- Pads queries to fixed-size buckets (256/512/1024 bytes)
- Adds random timing jitter
- Sends to SearXNG (optionally through Tor)
- Strips HTML, detects prompt injection in results, validates URLs
- Returns sanitized results + pre-formatted LLM context string
- Writes tamper-evident hash-chained audit log (JSONL)

## API

- `GET  /health`        -- health check + SearXNG status
- `POST /v1/search`     -- sanitized search
- `GET  /v1/search/test` -- SearXNG connectivity test

## Configuration (env vars)

- BIND_ADDR (default 127.0.0.1:8485)
- SEARXNG_URL (default http://127.0.0.1:8888)
- POLICY_PATH (optional, YAML policy for search settings)
- AUDIT_DIR (default /var/lib/llm-search-mediator/logs)

## Use cases

- Local AI assistants with web search, RAG pipelines, privacy-focused
  AI applications, any system where LLMs need web access without leaking
  user data

## Tech

- Python 3.10+, Flask, requests, pyyaml
- ~700 lines of code
- 28 unit tests (PII stripping, injection detection, result sanitization)
- Part of SecAI OS (https://github.com/SecAI-Hub/SecAI_OS)
